text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, defender is now Confused.

flavor_text.en: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to Flareon in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

rules_text.en: If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer affected by a Special Condition. Full Heal Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any. Potion Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)
Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can play this card only when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.
Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)
Search your deck for an Evolution card with Dark in its name. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Ask your opponent if he or she accepts your challenge. If your opponent declines (or if both Benches are full), draw 2 cards. If your opponent accepts, each of you searches your decks for any number of Basic Pokémon cards and puts them face down onto your Benches. (A player can't do this if his or her Bench is full.) When you both have finished, shuffle your decks and turn those cards face up.
Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.

Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be affected by Special Conditions. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play. You can put Pokémon that evolve from Mysterious Fossil on this card.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavor_text.en: Under a full moon, this Pokémon like to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.

rules_text.en: Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavor_text.en: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: It accumulates negative ions from the atmosphere to blast out 10,000-volt lightning bolts.
Using its amazing muscles, it throws powerful punches that can knock its victim clear over the horizon.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.
This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

text.en: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.
Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavor_text.en: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavor_text.en: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavor_text.en: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

text.en: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Colorless, this attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon of the same type as the Defending Pokémon (including your own).

flavor_text.en: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

text.en: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

text.en: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Golem does 100 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Its boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.

text.en: Look at up to 3 cards from the top of either player's deck and rearrange them as you like.
If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavor_text.en: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

text.en: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavor_text.en: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

text.en: Remove a number of damage counters from Kabutops equal to half the damage done to Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Kabutops has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavor_text.en: Its sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.

text.en: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)
Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 100 damage to itself.

flavor_text.en: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and attach it to another of your opponent's Pokémon of your choice.

Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavor_text.en: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

text.en: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

flavor_text.en: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

text.en: You may discard any number of R Energy cards attached to Moltres when you use this attack. If you do, discard that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavor_text.en: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavor_text.en: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

text.en: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavor_text.en: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

text.en: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

flavor_text.en: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

text.en: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.

flavor_text.en: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

text.en: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)

flavor_text.en: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

flavor_text.en: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

flavor_text.en: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

flavor_text.en: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

flavor_text.en: A Pokémon with a negative attitude, it hides in its shell whenever it's in a bad mood—which is often.

flavor_text.en: An aggressive Pokémon that tackles anything it sees.

flavor_text.en: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

flavor_text.en: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

flavor_text.en: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

flavor_text.en: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.

flavor_text.en: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

flavor_text.en: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

text.en: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavor_text.en: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

flavor_text.en: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything flat in moments.

flavor_text.en: A Pokémon capable of controlling water, so it is capable of creating giant whirlpools.

flavor_text.en: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

flavor_text.en: Burrow deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.
The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.
Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

flavor_text.en: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.
